meta.name: Refried Beans (Tex-Mex)
meta.subtitle: Creamy, savory beans for tacos, burritos, nachos, bowls, and sides.

source.name: SSA Canonical Tex-Mex
notes: Designed to work from scratch (dried beans) or quick (canned).
Includes lard/neutral oil + onion/garlic + cumin/chili for classic Tex-Mex flavor.

summary: Creamy refried beans with onion, garlic, cumin, and chili. Make from dried pinto beans for best flavor or use canned for a fast weeknight version.

notes: creamy
savory
cumin_garlic
toasty_chili

optional.label: Pressure cooker / Instant Pot
optional.notes: Speeds up cooking dried beans significantly.

ingredients.label: Dried pinto beans
ingredients.notes: About 2 cups dried. For quick version, see canned option below.

ingredients.label: Water
ingredients.notes: For boiling beans; may need more depending on pot and simmer time.

ingredients.label: Kosher salt (for cooking + final seasoning)
ingredients.notes: Start with 1 tsp during simmer, then adjust at the end.

ingredients.label: Lard (traditional) OR neutral oil
ingredients.notes: Use lard for classic flavor; use neutral oil for vegetarian/vegan.

ingredients.label: Chili powder
ingredients.notes: Adjust to taste; keep mild for family-friendly beans.

ingredients.label: Reserved bean cooking liquid (or broth)
ingredients.notes: Use more as needed to reach your preferred creaminess.

ingredients.label: Canned pinto beans (quick option)
ingredients.notes: 15 oz cans. If using canned, reduce total time and use water/broth to thin.

ingredients.label: Lime juice (optional)
ingredients.notes: Adds brightness; optional for a more traditional, savory-only profile.

ingredients.label: Chipotle pepper in adobo, minced (optional)
ingredients.notes: For smoky-spicy beans; keep optional so base stays broadly useful.

steps.title: Sort and rinse the beans
instructions: Spread dried pinto beans on a tray and pick out any stones or damaged beans.
Rinse beans under cool water until the water runs mostly clear.

steps.title: Cook beans until tender
instructions: Add beans and water to a pot. Bring to a boil, then reduce to a steady simmer.
Add 1 tsp salt. Partially cover and simmer until beans are very tender (about 90–120 minutes), adding water if needed.
When tender, reserve 2 cups of cooking liquid, then drain beans (or keep them in a small amount of liquid if you prefer).

steps.title: Sauté onion and garlic in fat
instructions: Heat lard (or neutral oil) in a skillet or saucepan over medium heat.
Add diced onion and cook until soft and lightly golden, 4–6 minutes.
Add garlic and cook 30–60 seconds until fragrant (don’t brown the garlic).
checks: Onion softened; garlic fragrant, not browned

steps.title: Toast spices briefly
instructions: Stir in cumin, chili powder (optional), oregano (optional), and black pepper (optional).
Cook 20–30 seconds to bloom spices.

steps.title: Add beans, mash, and adjust texture
instructions: Add cooked beans to the pan. Stir to coat in the onion-garlic-spice mixture.
Mash with a masher until mostly smooth (leave some texture if you like).
Add reserved bean liquid a splash at a time until creamy and spreadable (typically 1–1 1/2 cups).
Simmer 2–4 minutes, stirring, until thickened to your preference.

steps.title: Season and finish
instructions: Taste and adjust salt (add remaining 1 tsp gradually as needed).
Optional: add lime juice for brightness, or a little adobo/chipotle for smoky heat.
Serve warm as a side or as a filling/spread for Tex-Mex meals.

steps.title: Quick canned-bean option (skip dried-bean cooking)
instructions: If using canned beans: sauté onion/garlic in fat as above, toast spices, then add 2 cans drained pinto beans.
Add 3/4 cup water or broth, mash, and simmer 3–5 minutes until creamy.
Season to taste (salt is often lower/higher depending on brand—add gradually).

notes: Store in an airtight container.
Reheat with a splash of water or broth to restore creaminess.
Cool completely before freezing.
Freeze in flat bags or portion containers; thaw overnight in fridge and reheat with added liquid.
Cool beans quickly (spread in a shallow container) before refrigerating.
Reheat to steaming hot; avoid leaving at room temp longer than 2 hours.

batchPrep: Cook a full pound of beans and portion into 1-cup containers for quick taco nights.
Use as a base component for burritos, bowls, and nachos throughout the week.

notes: Double batch: use a wider pan for mashing to avoid scorching; add liquid gradually.
For ultra-smooth restaurant-style beans: use an immersion blender after mashing.
Swap pinto beans for black beans; keep cumin, add a pinch of coriander if desired.
Finish with lime for a brighter profile.
Add 1 tsp adobo sauce and 1/2 to 1 chipotle pepper minced during final seasoning.
Balance heat with a tiny pinch of honey if needed.
Use neutral oil instead of lard.
Skip cheese toppings; garnish with cilantro/onion or a dairy-free crema.
